texto: It has been said at various points in history that the current sum of humanknowledge is but a fraction of that which was once known, yet now is lost.Likewise, it is argued with simple mathematics that any sum of knowledge wemay yet accrue must always equate to virtually nothing when compared to theinfinity of what is. Apparently our's is a fate of perpetual ignorance. What then is truly lost in the course of human events?(attrib: 'R.I.B. Ushguriud', Note On The Text)
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robert Robert que provoca temas ligados a sabedoria. Ao questionar “It has been said at various points in history that the current sum of humanknowledge is but a fra…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, history, ignorance, com foco em ignorance e knowledge.

texto: I don't know what you mean, but I know that you mean it.(attrib: 'Edward', Appendix 2)
explicacao: Citação de Robert Robert sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I don't know what you mean, but I know that you mean it.(attrib: 'Edward', Appendix 2)” articula reflexao, meaning, attrib em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.
